description:
Cosmetic vocab cleanup surfaced by bl-05f9 (closed no-op). Spec §0 retires the trail/terminus/operating *discovery model* (anchor->trail->step->terminus->operating symlink + next:-chain) — that mechanism is fully GONE. But the §8 git-SEAL seam still NAMES itself with two of the retired words: the git::Terminus trait (src/git.rs: the ref you open a worktree off / seal+integrate onto / reset) and 'operating' (the checkout path/field, e.g. Git{operating}, operating_clone fixtures). These are a legitimate CURRENT concept, NOT residual trail code — but reusing retired words means a future reader greps 'terminus'/'operating' (~80 hits) and mistakes the live seal seam for the dead discovery model (exactly the mis-scope bl-05f9 nearly hit).

SCOPE: rename only — pick non-retired §8 vocab the spec already uses for this seam (seal/integrate/target-branch/store-checkout; e.g. Terminus->Seal or Anvil, operating->checkout/store). Touches src/git.rs, lifecycle.rs, mutate.rs, checkout.rs, delivery*.rs, registry.rs, verb.rs + their test files. No behavior change; tests must pass unchanged in substance. Spec §8 (architecture.md:508) doesn't prescribe a name, so update §8 doc-comments to match.

WHY low priority / deferred: cross-cutting across many files = high conflict risk with concurrent agents on bl-72a8 phases. Land it during a quiet window, NOT mid-fanout. See memory project_terminus_operating_are_seal_vocab.

issue.last_synced_title: Rename §8 git-seal vocab: Terminus trait / operating path off the retired discovery words [bl-2962]

title: Rename §8 git-seal vocab: Terminus trait / operating path off the retired discovery words
